title: phlomoides glabra
intro: Phlomoides glabra, also known as Indian snakeroot, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat snake bites and other poisonings, due to its content of iridoid glycosides and sesquiterpenes. The health benefits of Phlomoides glabra include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relieve muscle cramps and spasms, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
